notes: The Latin major is designed to identify and analyze ancient classical documents, material evidence, or other forms of primary sources and demonstrate their critical skills by engaging in presentations and weekly discourse with their peers. Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major. To be considered for this major, you should complete the following courses by the end of spring before transfer: One year of college level coursework in the declared language OR One year of high school in which the primary language of instruction was the declared language. Completion of two years of college foreign language is STRONGLY RECOMMENDED . A placement exam is required for all language majors regardless of the credit earned in lower division language courses. All courses must be taken for a letter grade. For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https://classics.ucla.edu and https:// admission.ucla.edu . PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses.
